note: Often warns of danger or tricks.
Step in the mud — a blunder.
Can't get footing with the ball.

spanish: tirar la casa por la ventana
note: Throw the house out the window.
No grandmother to brag for you.
Short for better late than never.
Short for cost an arm and a leg.
Put the dots on the i's.
Not have hairs on the tongue.
Up to the crown of the head.
Same as ser pan comido — easy.

spanish: cría cuervos y te sacarán los ojos
note: cría cuervos = raise crows = to raise someone who later betrays you.
Of the king of Rome — they appear.
The devil knows more from being old than devil.
Put your hand in the fire for it.
Come out a frog — bad surprise.
Give it the rug sweep — end discussion.
